name: dataverse-sample-data-generator
description: Generate realistic sample data for any Microsoft Dataverse environment. Works standalone or with GitHub Copilot Chat.

enumDescriptions: Browser sign-in (recommended — supports MFA, SSO, federation)
Device code flow (fallback for restricted environments)
App registration (Client ID + Client Secret, no user context)

dvdata.clientId.description: Azure AD App Registration Client ID

dvdata.maxRecordsPerRun.description: Maximum total records per generation run (safety limit)

chatParticipants.name: dvdata
chatParticipants.description: Generate sample data for Dataverse tables

commands.name: tables
commands.description: List available tables in the connected environment

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Show execution plan for data generation (dry run)

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate and insert sample data into Dataverse

disambiguation.description: The user wants to generate, create, or insert sample, test, or demo data into a Microsoft Dataverse environment, Power Platform, or Dynamics 365 tables.
examples: Generate 500 sample contacts in my Dataverse environment
Create test data for Account and Contact tables
I need realistic demo data for my Power Apps
Fill my dev environment with sample records
Generate data for Dynamics 365 tables with proper relationships

languageModelTools.name: dvdata_generate_sample_data
languageModelTools.userDescription: Generate and insert realistic sample data into Microsoft Dataverse tables with proper relationships
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generates realistic sample data and inserts it into Microsoft Dataverse (Power Platform / Dynamics 365) tables. Reads table metadata dynamically, resolves dependencies and relationships between tables, and bulk-inserts records using the Dataverse Web API. Supports configurable record counts per table, seeded random generation for reproducibility, and scenario prompts for domain-specific data (e.g., 'finance company', 'healthcare clinic'). Requires an active Dataverse connection. Returns a summary of created records, any errors, and a run manifest.

tables.description: List of Dataverse table logical names to generate data for (e.g., ['account', 'contact']). If empty, the user will be prompted to select tables.

recordCount.description: Number of records to generate per table. Defaults to 50 if not specified.

seed.description: Random seed for reproducible data generation. Optional.

scenario.description: Scenario prompt for domain-specific realistic data (e.g., 'finance company in New York', 'healthcare clinic in London'). Optional.
